title: EU AIFMD Qualified Investor Standards
content:
This document establishes Meridian Wealth Partners' internal standards for assessing client eligibility for Alternative Investment Fund (AIF) products under the EU Alternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (AIFMD) Article 43 and related MiFID II suitability requirements. All senior advisors and private wealth managers must adhere to these qualification thresholds when recommending aggressive alternative investment strategies to European clients.

PROFESSIONAL INVESTOR CLASSIFICATION: Clients must meet the quantitative thresholds under MiFID II Annex II to qualify as professional investors. This includes institutional investors with minimum capital of €125,000 for credit institutions, €730,000 for investment firms, or €1,000,000 for insurance companies. For individual clients seeking professional status, they must satisfy at least two of three criteria: (1) portfolio size exceeding €500,000, (2) transaction frequency of at least 10 trades per quarter in relevant markets over the previous four quarters, and (3) professional experience in the financial sector for at least one year in positions requiring knowledge of alternative investments.

AIF-SPECIFIC SUITABILITY ASSESSMENT: Beyond professional investor status, clients investing in AIFs with aggressive risk profiles must demonstrate sophisticated understanding of complex financial instruments. Advisors must document client comprehension of: leverage ratios typically ranging from 2:1 to 8:1, illiquidity periods extending 3-7 years for private equity and real estate funds, and potential total loss scenarios inherent in hedge fund strategies. Minimum investment thresholds for most AIF products range from €100,000 to €1,000,000, with some institutional-grade funds requiring €5,000,000 minimum commitments.

RISK CAPACITY AND CONCENTRATION LIMITS: Under our enhanced due diligence framework, alternative investments should not exceed 20% of a client's total investable assets, with individual AIF positions capped at 5% unless exceptional circumstances warrant higher allocation. Clients must maintain liquid reserves equivalent to at least 6 months of living expenses outside their alternative investment portfolio. For ultra-high-net-worth clients with portfolios exceeding €10,000,000, concentration limits may be relaxed to 30% total alternatives exposure upon senior management approval.

DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENTS: All AIF recommendations require completion of Form MPW-AIF-01 Alternative Investment Suitability Assessment, including detailed risk tolerance questionnaire, investment experience verification, and signed acknowledgment of illiquidity risks. Advisors must obtain updated financial statements within 90 days of investment for clients with net worth below €2,000,000. Annual suitability reviews are mandatory for all AIF holdings, with interim reviews triggered by material changes in client circumstances or fund performance deviations exceeding ±25% from benchmark.

COMPLIANCE MONITORING: The Compliance team conducts quarterly reviews of all new AIF investments, focusing on adherence to concentration limits and proper documentation. Any deviations from these standards require immediate escalation to the Chief Compliance Officer and may result in suspension of AIF sales privileges. Senior advisors must complete annual AIFMD training updates and maintain current knowledge of regulatory developments affecting alternative investment sales practices across all EU member states where Meridian operates.
